name: Bernie Privin
abstract: Bernard Privin (February 12, 1919 – October 8, 1999) was an American jazz trumpeter.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Bernie Privin as someone they used to work with. The interviewee (Cliff Leeman) does not mention Bernie Privin in their response, nor does he confirm a personal or professional relationship with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: We had ... Bernie Privin who was with Artie Shaw ... So you know, we tried to put as many of these great musicians to work
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: Most of the guys on here were with The World's Greatest Jazz Band, except Bernie Privin, of course, and Bernie Layton.

about_quotes.speaker: John Best
about_quotes.evidence: He thought my tone didn’t blend with Chuck Peterson and Bernie Privin.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Best interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: you had Bernie Privin later on ... that’s why I liked the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
